name: Money
description_short: Money Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit credit union serving members across 44 U.S. states and 5 countries with competitive loan rates, multiple savings accounts, and member-focused banking.
description_long:
Money Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union headquartered in Syracuse, NY. As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, the institution operates on a cooperative model where members share in profits through higher savings rates and lower loan rates. The organization explicitly positions itself as an alternative to traditional banking, emphasizing relationship-building over profit maximization.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products including checking and savings accounts (with nine distinct savings account options), auto loans, home improvement loans, home equity lines of credit with no closing costs, private student loans, personal loans, and youth accounts for ages 1-19. They also provide business checking accounts designed to help small businesses avoid high fees associated with traditional banks. All loan products are marketed with competitive rates and quick funding.

Key distinguishing factors include their emphasis on accessibility and member service. Money FCU operates across 44 U.S. states and 5 countries, allowing geographically dispersed members to conduct banking remotely with minimal branch visits. They advertise an average call answer time of under 12 seconds and highlight member testimonials praising their customer service friendliness. The credit union explicitly markets itself as a "family" environment focused on celebrating member successes.

As a credit union, Money FCU is NCUA-insured (not FDIC-insured), which means deposits are protected by the National Credit Union Administration. Members must meet eligibility requirements to join, though the website does not detail specific membership criteria. While offering competitive products and emphasizing service quality, the credit union's membership requirements and potential eligibility restrictions may limit access compared to traditional banks open to all customers.
services: Nine styles of interest-earning savings accounts
Auto loans (new, used, and lease buyout financing)
Home equity lines of credit with no closing costs
Personal loans for debt consolidation, remodeling, and general purposes
Private student loans for education expenses
Credit union family membership across multiple states and countries
pros: Nine different savings account options to fit various savings goals and lifestyles
No closing costs on home equity lines of credit
Serves members in 44 U.S. states and 5 countries with online-first banking
Average call answer time under 12 seconds
Competitive rates across all loan products (auto, home, personal, student)
Kids accounts (ages 1-19) with reduced fees and educational benefits
Not-for-profit structure means higher savings rates and lower loan rates than traditional banks
cons: Membership eligibility requirements not clearly explained on website (typical for credit unions)
Limited branch locations compared to major national banks despite online capability
Private student loans require partnership arrangement and may have limitations on loan amounts
No specific APR ranges or rate quotes provided on website for loan products
Youth account program only covers ages 1-19, excluding older adults wanting entry-level accounts
best_for: Residents of Syracuse, NY area or those in 44 U.S. states seeking membership-based banking
Borrowers seeking competitive auto loans, home equity lines, and personal loans with transparent not-for-profit pricing
Savers looking for multiple account options to optimize interest earnings
Parents wanting to teach children financial literacy through youth accounts with fewer fees
diagnosis: Money Federal Credit Union is best for members seeking relationship-focused, not-for-profit banking with competitive rates and flexible savings options, provided they meet membership eligibility requirements. The main caveat is that as a credit union, membership may have restrictions (not detailed on website) and you must qualify to join, unlike traditional banks open to anyone.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Money — Credit Union in Syracuse, NY | CreditDoc
meta_description: Money — NCUA-insured credit union in Syracuse, NY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
